page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, which provides 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2172.0 and a text processing score of 2822.0, both placing in the top tier. Single-threaded compression and decompression are also strong, with gzip compression reaching 34.54 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 14.48 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is a notable tradeoff, as shown by the stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 4334.16 and a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 28.94 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also weak at 321.22 GB/sec, though large block read and write speeds perform at an average level.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i-flex.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads without the premium of local NVMe storage or GPU hardware. The inclusion of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions, enables efficient execution of vector and matrix operations. This makes the instance suitable for workloads such as single-threaded applications, text processing, static web serving, Redis databases, and lightweight LLM prompt processing, while it is less optimal for highly parallelized multi-threaded compute tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 2 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. The instance exhibits strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2172.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-threaded performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded benchmarks scoring in the bottom 25%. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for workloads such as text processing, static web serving, Redis operations, and light machine learning inference, while being less suited for highly parallelized computing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, offering strong single-core performance but weaker multi-threaded capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and strong single-core performance for text processing and web serving.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations via Intel AMX and AVX-512
Best suited for web serving, Redis, and single-threaded applications
Tradeoff of weaker multi-threaded performance and no local storage
